result.finding: The application has minimal security implementation. There is no Spring Security, JWT, OAuth2, HTTPS/TLS configuration, or authorization logic. The application runs over plain HTTP on port 8080 with all Actuator endpoints publicly exposed. The only security-relevant concern is that the MySQL password is externalized as an environment variable (MYSQL_PASSWORD) but defaults to an empty string.
evidence: No JWT, OAuth2, or Basic Auth configuration found
No @EnableWebSecurity, @PreAuthorize, or @Secured annotations in source code
management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=* — all Actuator endpoints exposed without authentication
MYSQL_PASSWORD defaults to empty string in application-mysql.properties
values: Risk: All Spring Boot Actuator endpoints publicly accessible without authentication
